review_text: Don't EVER do business with this shop. I've taken my car in FOUR times for the same issue and they still can't figure it out after I paid nearly $400 for the service.They are refusing to grant me a refund unless I bring it in again.Their staff, especially Rachel, is rude and condescending and apparently cares more about making a buck than providing quality service.Is $400 really worth the damage to your reputation?By the way: A business that still doesn't have soap and a dispenser in the men's room for an ENTIRE MONTH after being notified by a customer should tell you all you need to know about its professionalism, attention to detail, customer service and the business culture - not to mention hygiene.UPDATE: AnA Tire and Automotive finally did refund the money after I posted this review. Meanwhile, I took my car to a competitor who accurately diagnosed and fixed the problem within hours AND diagnosed and fixed two other major issues AnA either missed or ignored. Total cost? $133.

Do not use this place. I took my vehicle for inspection. Had been there before. They failed my car saying it had low fluids and cracks in the Serpentine belt. I was pretty sure the fluids could not have been low since I had just taken it for 30,000 mile inspection at the dealership not a month before. I checked DMV website. It says they may NOT fail a vehicle for cracks in the Serpentine Belt. So, took it to third place. Fluids were not low and cracks in Serpentine belt were so minor ! Vhicle passed inspection with flying colors! I sent a letter to danny's with copies of all back up but feceived NO response or effort to correct their "error" Do not be be scammed by this place.

My husband took his vehicle to this shop.  They did not fix it.  Jason was the one helping me.  Don't go here.  They just took our money and still having  the same problems.  You never see Mr. David.  It's like he is a Ghost.Remember Don't fool with THIS SHOP.  PLEASE.

If Alexander R from Discount Tire sent you, TURN AROUND AND GO BACK HOME. Alexander R sent me here after I purchased two tires for $392 from him. After waiting for an hour he explains that he couldn't install the tires because he didn't have the key for the wheel lock and he sent me to this shop to buy one. "It shouldn't be too much" he assured me. Well, Alexander lied. Not only did the unenthusiastic receptionist, Jessica, not know what I was talking about, but she did little to explain what they were going to do to my vehicle. She took my keys and asked me to write my information. After waiting around for 30 minutes I began to worry. What are they doing to my car? By this time Jessica had already gone to get her lunch and a young man took over for her who was even more clueless than she was. When one of the car salesmen started talking to me, he explained that they had to rip the locks off and that it was going to cost $100. At checkout Jessica charged me $123. This was a lot more than "Not too much" as Alexander R had originally said. My frustration with this establishment is with their lack of communication and transparency in regard to pricing and what they were actually doing with my vehicle.
I think this place has been sold. Had work done a few years ago and no problems, now I call several times and leave messages but no call back. Took my business elsewhere.

Overall the service to the car was good, but the customer service was HORRIBLE. I understand that my particular situation was complex, but it would have been nice to have that communicated to me by staff. Instead, I had to keep calling numerous times for updates and it seemed like no one knew what was going on. The hold up was that they were waiting for the part. The manager (David) finally called me to inquire if anyone has been calling me/for an update, and I told him no one called. I only received one voicemail. He then told me that one of his staff members (Reuben) said that they could not get a hold of me when he clearly left a voicemail previously. Bottom line if you are going to take longer than a day or two on someone's car, tell them that so other arrangements can be made. This business had my car for a week with only two business initiated phone calls as updates. Then on top of that, when I get my car back they tell me of all this other work that needs to be done, and GO FORWARD WITH A SERVICE I DID NOT AUTHORIZE (ultimately making the total bill almost $200+ dollars than what I was originally quoted). Frankly, I feel that I was taken advantaged of at this place. I never want to come back nor do I recommend them. I don't like to seem negative, but this has been my first and last experience with the company.

My windshield wiper motor is failing. So I went to them this week on Monday, they said to come back Wednesday. I come back Wednesday they say come back today (Thursday). Then I come back, they say just a few hours only to diagnose the problem with a $200 fee. I have never been charged such a high fee for a diagnosis. I wait hours upon hours with no call back or update on the situation with my car so I decide to go back in person. They tell me to wait in the lobby for 5 minutes, 20 minutes later they tell me that to fix the windshield wipers it's $600-$800 oh but with a "discount" of $100. So trying to keep my composure, I try to get my car back and they said since they opened up too much (for literally windshield wipers...) they won't have it ready until tomorrow morning. As I'm walking out the door trying not to cause a scene, someone from the front desk asks if I would want to work together to come to a better price point. You guys held no hesitation in screwing me over price-wise for such a small matter the first time paired with terrible customer service, why would I trust you in any negotiation process? This is one of the rare off days I have and even if my windshield wipers don't work, I would have at least wanted access to drive. This might be the worst experience I've had at an auto place, and they go against everything that you'll hear them stand for through an automated message when you're on hold with them forever. If I could leave them zero or negative stars, I would. Please save your time and money.

I recently received an oil change here. This was my second time here, the first time was great! I'm disappointed to say that the second time wasn't so great. They did my oil change without informing me the were going to change the oil grade. When I asked the owner why was a different oil put in, he said I received the "special". I never asked for the special, and I wouldn't have minded paying more for the oil my car initially had. I just think that before ANYTHING was altered or changed I should have been informed. The owner is a very nice, but my review is for the lack of communication on their behalf.

Horrific experience. Thousands of dollars down the drain. Car in shop for 5 months for a weeks work. Excuses and lies on delays. Car returned in worst condition than when taken in. Brakes squeal after brake job. AC not working as they couldn't fix it. Refunded $123 after charging $700 for that part of the non repair on A/C. Soft top on convertible which worked when brought in no longer functions. Piece of door broken and left on front seat when returned. Misdiagnosed repairs. Left multiple messages for owner / manager to call and nothing. Left owner vm on cell phone no return calls. They don't give a damn. Car at reputable shop now assessing damages these savages did. Got BBB incol

Pains me to do so, as each and every employee I've met here is extremely nice, however my most recent service here was beyond disappointing. First, it's extremely hard to get an appointment here. I understand this is a very popular business, but perhaps an assistant or two to respond to text messages, inquiries, etc. would be helpful. Additionally, it was like pulling teeth to receive an emailed quote, prior to scheduling an appointment, although it's something they say they are willing to provide. Those issues I have ignored. But most recently after receiving repairs on my vehicle I noticed the service light was still on. I was confused and assumed it would take a few miles to turn off; it never did. Zameer actually reached out to inquire on my satisfaction with service and I told him that my service light was still on and asked if it would turn off by itself. No response! To make matters worse, not long after service, my car starts acting psychotic. Come to find out, via another mechanic, that one of my batteries is completely drained. Nothing indicated that my battery was dying when I got a Service B? This was only in November and there is nothing I've done differently since receiving service. Trust me, I know how to not drain my battery. And since my service light never turned off, never received a response from Zameer re why my service light was still showing AND the fact that my car is now having issues, I wont be returning. I've been driving from north Houston because when I find a business I like, I'm extremely loyal, so this is unfortunate. But at least I was able to locate a great mechanic who is closer to my residence. No more driving an hour for a business that clearly didn't care about my "satisfaction". Like I said, nice group of folks, but customer service and communication could be better. Think about it; if I received a response at the very least, I wouldn't be updating my review.

I've called in regards to a having my sunroof seal replaced and some work on the dash of my Tahoe for the past 6 months. I've left my information asked for calls back 3-4 times with no response. If the work can't be done I understand but the lack of communication is frustrating.

Hello Blake, I apologize for the unpleasant experience you had with us. As you can see from all of our 5 Star reviews we do our very best to take care of every customer. I would like the opportunity to get it taken care of for you if you still need the service. We did look at it for you at no charge and provided an estimated based on what we did find to be the issue. No excuse for the lack of communication on our side. As the owner, I should have followed up as well as or our service advisor should have described the repair process to you. Sunroof repairs are tricky and a lot of shops do avoid them because of that very reason. I will be reaching out to you, but also please feel free to reach out to me direct at 713-480-5533. I have my cell phone number on our business cards in our lobby so I can be contacted directly in any situation.-Zameer (Owner)

I was referred to this shop by my family when I moved to Stafford. The first repair was in 2019 that cost $1000+. I cannot find the receipt but this particular repair I was quite satisfied. And not once have I questioned your diagnosis and charges. Mr. Saqib, I may not be your top $$ customer so I may not be a loss to  you. Sorry, I no longer want to continue doing business with your shop. I have attached photos of all the repairs done with my 2010 Nissan. Total repairs totaled to almost $4500 within months. My vehicle has been properly maintained since we used to own a repair shop since 1998 in Houston till we sold it. Within months apart my sensorS were replaced, my engine light would keep showing code P0171 from your scanner. My car would be @ your shop for hours. Your tech will turn off the engine light but I returned days after cuz it's back on. This happened 3x I keep driving back showing the same code.Svc Adv. : sensor may be defective. So I left my car again Your staff said they replaced the defective sensor (?) how can I know they did, but I trusted your shop. Then the eng light came back less than a week. This kept happening. On my 4th diagnosis same P0171, your svc adv quoted me $700 cuz he said I NOW have a vacuum leak  Bank 1 (in writing). I had to take Uber to work again. My vehicle was there for a day and a half again.  They did not notice the leak in the past 3 visits? I decided to get a 2nd opinion. I drove 17 miles with my engine light ON to the shop our family used to go to in Spring branch for years. Within minutes, the mechanic showed me the hose/line/pipe that connects to the sensor was DISCONNECTED. He showed me as he reconnects it and confirms there is NO LEAK . The engine light has never been lit since. They did not charge me.I will give your tech the benefit of the doubt the he FORGOT to reconnect it and not think maliciously after several visits.  But when I was quoted $700 to a repair that was NOT necessary at all, I looked back now at th

Save yourself the frustration and avoid this place! I brought my car here to get the A/ C repaired based off the good reviews. They had my car for two days and still couldn't tell me where the leak was coming from, with my A/C. The communication throughout the experience was terrible, and I felt like I was getting the runaround every time I talked to someone over the phone. I went to the shop to speak with someone directly about an hour before closing time on the second day and they had already closed for the day.  They still charged me for the diagnostic with no results, stating that the mechanic would need more time to find the leak. At that point even if they found the leak there was no way I was going to trust them to repair it. Ultimately, I wouldn't recommend them because they have poor communication and time management skills

Bad experience.The air conditioning in my 2005 C240 Mercedes conked out.The cable to the emergency park also got disconnected.Left my car there Wednesday April 10.They said they called Oklahoma for parts.The establishment gave me a loaner car which unfortunately could not be found in the neighborhood where I went to on April 12.Calls to towing services and to HPD were to no avail.So now they are using my insurance to claim the loss.If it is a loaner car don't they have their own insurance?Then they called me today and I was thinking the service was already done.Made me pay $3400 but the car is not ready coz they said they are waiting parts from Germany.I also had to pay $200 down payment for a metal rim for the spare tire.It is also in transit.What I am wondering about is why did I have to pay upfront when NOTHING has been done on the car yet?And I have to rent a car until God knows when.I have been in Houston for a year and when I was in College Station I only had to pay for the service when the car is pick-up ready and really done.

Took my car in because battery was sluggish. They confirmed I needed a new battery. Under warranty from AAA, which Montrose Auto is a certified provider, the battery was replaced by roadside assistance, whom Montrose Tire told me to contact. Montrose Tire - sole service provider for my family's three cars for the past decade - wanted to charge nearly $100 for the battery diagnosis, which took about 1 minute. If I had ignored my warranty and bought a new battery, the charge would not be applied. When I said this charge was exorbitant, they asked me why I brought it in if I didn't expect to be charged. I said because you are my mechanic .... I have bought batteries, tires and annual inspections as a loyal longtime customer. Heck, Discount Tire plugs a tire leak for free even if you aren't a customer! Cost them my business and recommendation. This never would happened under Lee's management.

Quick Read: Be cautious about this place. I had multiple parts replaced at once with Jim and his guys and one by one all of the parts failed in some fashion. Full Description: I'm not sure what occured the opposition on the first visit, but Jim told me I would only get into trouble by using tools on my vehicle. I ignored his rudeness and decided to come back based on the reviews on yelp. Jim stated, "you shouldn't have come back!" For which I replied, "you've got amazing reviews on Yelp!" The water pump (one of many parts) failed within months after the replacement. They also stripped a bolt for the water pump, but they atleast got it on! Now it's difficult to get off! They've cut factory zip ties and didn't replace them (this may not seem like much, but take into account that if they're willing to take short cuts and strip bolts then what?). I can't say for certain that it was this company as I brought my own parts which were of medium to high end quality. In other words, these parts weren't the cheapest available. However, I just can't understand how all of the parts that I replaced at Hare Repair failed based statistically. Maybe one part failing I can understand, but all of them (and only from Hare Repair...)... I just don't know.
Asked questions.  Answered quickly and hung up without giving me a chance to respond.

called on friday, requested a service for saturday. they confirmed that they would work on it saturday and it should be done by close of business that day.drop the car off at 9:45 saturday morning. i received no call on saturday to let me know what was going on. i called on monday morning 8 AM, they asked me to call back in 15 minutes. no one answered the phone for the next 6 hours. finally got an answer at 4 pm. they couldn't tell me if they had even looked at my vehicle yet. arrived at the shop, they hadn't even looked at my car issue and couldn't find me on their "list". i literally had to point out to them where my name/phone number/car/issue was on their schedule. they had my car for over 3 days and hadn't even looked at it. complete waste of time. don't make my mistake.

DANGER!!!11/3/2021Dropped my truck off for diagnosis check. Found the "ISSUE" and declined the $1,500.00 service to fix my truck. (Truck was drivable with no check engine lights prior to visit)Hours later, oil and check engine lights have now come on and I'm currently stranded.I've tried calling to speak to the lead technician (owner) and he refused to speak to me.Update 11/5/2021Had a mechanic come inspect the truck and found that all the radiator fluid was drained out!!!! I didn't wait long but it make sense now as to why I didn't have a long wait time. After I paid, I began to drive my vehicle and this loud screeching noise began whenever I would turn my wheel, it was NOT making this noise prior. I called them and they stated to return. Tech IC, told the representative after he test drove my vehicle in the parking lot that he did not hear any noise, I told him I wouldn't be back here if that was the case. The tech said he would test drive it on the road and I said, I'll accompany him. Sure enough, the noise was loud enough to hear and he said oh yeah, I can hear it now "coincidentally," but a few minutes ago you told your boss you did not hear any noise. We return to the shop and he told me he would redo a power steering flush to assure everything is fine. Not even 5 minutes later, I see the car coming back and the representative from the front desk tells me that it's "normal" for the power steering system to have air in the system. Be mindful, I have gotten a power steering flush before at a ASE certified shop and this is not normal. They basically wanted to me to simply leave as I was telling the front desk representative what if the noise does not stop? He then says, just come back but that is what I did and you simply refused any accountability of the wrong doing. I even questioned the tech, didn't you just tell me you were going to complete a flush of the power steering system and he refused to answer me. I understand my vehicle is an older model vehicle but every vehicle needs to be handled as if it were yours. The past three times I brought in my vehicle, I had great experiences. I was recommended to this shop by a former tech but was shocked with todays visit. I then inspected the area the tech worked on and was shocked but not surprised by what I discovered. T

My Nightmare with a So-Called Mercedes Service CenterI made the grave mistake of bringing my Mercedes C250 to what I now consider the most incompetent service center imaginable for some electrical issues. What followed wasn't just disappointing--it was an absolute nightmare that dragged on for over a month of my life that I'll never get back.From the moment I reluctantly handed over my keys, the facility went completely dark. Not a single phone call. Not one text. Nothing. It was as if my expensive vehicle had vanished into a black hole. I called repeatedly, growing increasingly frustrated as my calls went straight to voicemail, my messages collecting dust. The deafening silence spoke volumes about how little they valued me as a customer.Desperate for any information, I wasted my time and gas driving to their facility multiple times, only to be met with blank stares and pathetic excuses. The staff seemed determined to keep me in the dark, offering vague, unhelpful responses that told me absolutely nothing about my car's condition. Their indifference was staggering.The only thing that finally prompted them to acknowledge my existence was my threat to have the vehicle towed elsewhere. Suddenly, they scrambled to share what little they had discovered during their month of doing practically nothing. When I demanded an estimate and timeline--basic information any reputable shop should provide--I received nothing but evasive non-answers: "I'm not sure how long" and "maybe around $2,000 but not any more." No specifics, no confidence, no competence.Thoroughly disgusted by their unprofessionalism, I had my Mercedes towed to another facility the very next day. But the insult wasn't over. These people had the audacity to demand $379 for what they called "diagnosis"--a laughable claim considering they had accomplished nothing in over a month. Even after I fought them down to $277, I felt completely ripped off.The final slap in the face? They returned my vehicle with a torn-up f

1 star isn't even close to accurate, but it's the lowest choice. I dropped my Lexus off 2 weeks ago. I made it clear multiple times that it was time sensitive because my lease is ending. I was assured of a timely fix. All body work, nothing wrong with the motor or anything. A few days later, I get a call that it's finished. I pay the deductible and when I get the car back it's only half finished. I point this out and they assure me (it's Friday) that it will be fixed by Monday. I Get no call on Monday, so I called on Tuesday. They say it can be picked up Thursday. They call me Thursday and I go get it. The manager says that he has personally looked at every fix with his own eyes. I take it home. and find that the under side bumper is still completely scratched up and hasn't been touched.So he lied right to my face. So I take it back and he makes an excuse that he has a bad back and can't get that low. So why tell me you looked at it then? So it has been 2 weeks and my lease is due back today at 5pm. I was told that it would finally be finished today at 12. I just got a call and was told it might be ready by 3. I'm not holding my breath. I have called the regional manager and left multiple messages over the last few days and have yet to get a return call. NEVER use this shop. Seriously. They could care less once they get your car in their hands.Update: finally had the regional manger contact me between 12 and 3 when I went for pickup. He was very nice, and apologized for not being aware sooner. He assured me that he'd do whatever he could to make things right, although he admitted that there wasn't many  options with such little time. He said that he'd make sure that we got set up with a night out for dinner on his company to say try to make up for poor service. Guess what... They never did that either. I didn't even care if they did that or not, but why offer if you aren't gonna follow through. Sadly, I wasn't surprised.

I tried sending a calm letter to the heads of the company.. since I really wanted to let them know how what I thought was a great company has escaped it's values.. but they ignored me. Then I emailed them and they ignored that. I figured that 60 visits would be worth at least a canned response.. guess not. -My car has 87,000 miles on it. It is the first car I have ever purchased and I'm proud to own it!  I continuously brought it to My Mechanic. I understood them to be a company that charges premium prices but the customer gets loyalty. I used to tell my friends "They charge a lot, but they don't rip you off". I felt that way forever. Today I got some information that makes me think the company got away from it's values... or just made a big mistake. Basically they charged me several times for a lightbulb when my car was repeatedly melting it. The last time I went there I paid an expensive fee to fix the wiring to resolve the melting lightbulb problem.I was told it would work, then it did work.Today they told me I need another lightbulb. They didn't manage that it got "melted" again until I asked.They also let me know that I had 18 month warranties on the bulb...  and they honored that only today.. not the previous times.When I asked about why they didn't use my warranty on the previous bulbs, they told me it's because my car was melting it.So I asked why they kept on letting me get new ones, melting the bulb, and then charging me for new ones, and then on MELT #3, they told me to do major work to avoid it... then they quietly tried to sell me a bulb today. Also today, although they "courteously" didn't charge me for the bulb, the price I paid was $78 for oil change plus state inspection and really confusing costs. $78 is kind of high for an oil change and inspection isn't it? I don't know.But this light bulb thing cost me about 4 visits, many hours, and just stress. I had to keep on driving on the road with one light, and it would always burn out.Today the manager

I'm honestly really upset about having to change this review. I thought I FINALLY found a reputable place to take my vehicles but once again, let down massively. First car I had taken a couple of times and one of those times they replaced the AC belt, it has been squeaking since they replaced it. I called to tell them about it the following day and we decided to get them to look at it next time I took it in. This review is about my experience bringing in my 2022 Chrysler Voyager to Master Garage.This was actually the second vehicle I brought in within a few days...the first being my 2022 RAV4 Hybrid. I initially believed everything went smoothly with the RAV4, which is why I returned so quickly with a second vehicle. Unfortunately, what happened with the Chrysler has caused me to question the transparency of both visits. I will be getting the RAV4 inspected separately and will post a separate review once I verify all work.1. Clearly Agreed-Upon Parts (With Screenshots):Before dropping off the Chrysler , Alex Moazeni and I exchanged texts confirming the exact brake pads to be used:-BrakeBest Select Pro Ceramic Pads-Part numbers: SPC1589 (front) and SPC1901 (rear)Screenshots attached show the full discussion and agreement.I even sent him the product links, the prices, and screenshots of the pads in the cart, and he confirmed that these were the pads he would go with because they "have a better warranty." Everything was documented in writing.2. Lower-Tier Pads Were Installed Instead (With Photos Provided by the Shop):After picking up the van, I inspected the brakes myself and saw completely different pads installed:-Total Stopping Solutions (TSS) ceramic pads-TD1589C front-TD1901C rearThese are not the BrakeBest pads we discussed.They're a lower-tier product and a significantly lower-priced one.The shop even sent me photos of the boxes (attached), confirming the mismatch.3. The Price Difference (Which Is Documented):Here are the actual numbers:-BrakeBest Select Pro Front: $84.99-BrakeBest Select Pro Rear: $78.99-TSS Front: $56.99-TSS Rear: $36.49Total difference: $76.32Yet I was charged the full amount associated with the higher-tier pads.4. No Communication Before Substitution:I was not called, texted, or asked permission to switch the agreed-upon product.The shop's explanations changed multiple times, including:-"They didn't have that brand."-"We paid the same price."-"I didn't know it would be that much of a concern."But when a shop cannot source the parts the customer agreed to, the standard practice is to call and ask before substituting. That did not happen.5. Warranty Claims That Weren't Accurate:During our conversation, Alex also used the "included warranty" as justification for the price and why a refund shouldn't be necessary.However:-Brake pad warranties come from the manufacturer, not from the shop.-The shop's responsibility is only to process the claim, not to pay for replacement pads out of pocket.-Manufacturers typically reimburse or credit the shop for warranty replacements.Meaning:-The warranty is not something the shop "includes."-The shop doesn't lose money by honoring it.-It cannot reasonably be used to justify installing cheaper pads at higher-tier pricing.This point had to be clarified directly.6. Attempts to Resolve the IssueTo his credit, Alex did respond, but the resolution process was frustrating:-First offered a free $18.50 state inspection-Then a free oil change-Then said "that's all we can do"-Then asked if an $80 refund was "fair"-Then asked if getting the refund meant I'd leave 5-star reviews on Google and Yelp(Screenshots attached). Offering a refund in exchange for a 5-star review is not only inappropriate, it violates FTC, Google, and Yelp policies.I clarified that any review would be honest and not tied to compensation.Alex eventually issued the $80 refund, which I appreciate, but only after a long back and forth that should not have been necessary considering the clear evidence.7. Now Questioning the RAV4 WorkBecause of how the Chrysler situation unfolded, I now feel obligated to have the RAV4 inspected to verify:-That the claimed work was actually performed-That the correct products were used-That the coolant flush was actually doneI will post a separate review once that inspection is complete.UPDATE 12/8/25: In response to the owner's response to my initial review...Alex, thank you for responding. Your reply actually reinforces exactly why I felt obligated to share my experience.To be clear, I did not request a 'huge discount.' I asked for the documented $76.32 difference between the BrakeBest Select Pro pads we agreed on and the lower-tier TSS pads that were installed without approval. You refunded that because the documentation was indisputable.More concerning than the parts issue is your choice to tell a customer who brought you two vehicles in one week that they 'can't afford to maintain their vehicle.' That comment says far more about the professionalism of this shop than anything in my review.My review is not emotional, it's supported by screenshots, part numbers, pricing, and your own written messages. Everything stated is accurate and backed by evidence.I'm not here to argue. My review exists so future customers can see the full experience and decide for themselves.

I'm sure Snappee Auto Care is fine for getting oil changes and inspection stickers but if your car needs any mechanical repairs, STAY FAR AWAY from this business.  I feel like their mechanic, Tony, completely misdiagnosed the problem my car was having and only provided a temporary fix to get me down the road after paying the bill (see story below).  The brief interactions I had with Tony left me feeling as if he was putting off performing repairs on my car so he could better service his regular Park Cities customers (ex: informing me that nothing is wrong with my car when he first looked at it (makes you wonder if he did truly look at it) and then never calling me to discuss his findings-- I always had to reach out to him).  A week and a half later after picking up my car from his shop for overheating issues, it overheats again.  I call to inform him of this and ask if he could work a little bit with me on the $550+ bill I paid since he didn't correct the overheating issue and the car is undriveable once again, and he becomes belligerent and starts screaming at me on the phone.  Never in my life have I ever encountered such a rude, unprofessional individual.  For a shop that has their lobby decorated with Christian items (He is risen cross in front window, I Am Second pamphlets scattered on the desk and tables, Christian music blaring through the lobby speakers, etc.), one would think that they would be treated a little more fairly and not completely ripped off.  Lesson learned.One fateful day, as I was turning into the parking lot for a dentist's appointment, I noticed steam bellowing out from under the hood of my car.  I quickly found a parking spot and got out of my car to investigate.  It appeared that a large amount of fluid had been released from the engine and was starting to pool around my front tires.  I immediately googled for a car repair shop nearby and discovered that Snappee Auto Care was less than a mile away from my location.  Since it was the closest repair shop to me, and after waiting an hour in the parking lot for my car to cool off, I made the short trek over.  Tony, the mechanic on duty at Snappee Auto Care, said he would take a look at my car tomorrow (car was still too hot from the drive over and they were getting ready to close).  The next morning Tony calls and exclaims that he "has good news, that I don't have a leak!" and that my car was ready for pick-up.  To which I reply, "Tony, I'm pretty sure I have a leak due to the large puddle of fluid that surrounded my car after parking it yesterday."  To make a long story short, he said he would run it idling for an hour and then get back to me.  He never got back to me.  Later in the day, I called him and asked if he found anything wrong with the car to which he nonchalantly states "Yeah, it's definitely overheating."  Eventually, after a pressure test and coolant flush, he determines the car needed a new cooling fan assembly which I agreed to have him repair.  I pick up my car a day later and pay the $550+ bill.  A WEEK AND A HALF later my low engine coolant light comes on.  I immediately take the car to Snappee Auto Care again to have Tony look at it.  He pops the hood and tells me the reservoir and fluid levels look fine and that there's nothing wrong with my car.  I drive away thinking everything is fine.  The NEXT DAY, while driving home from work, I notice my car overheating AGAIN and immediately take it to a mechanic's shop that's closer to my house where it's determined that there's a crack in the intake manifold.

I also recommend a transmission flush." I declined the transmission flush but approved the other services. I picked up my vehicle a few days later and SAME ISSUE with the timing (intermittent start/ long crank with rattling noises). When I looked at my work order/receipt it said "Camshaft adjusters possibly need to be replaced." I called the shop and talked to Tony and he literally said "We replace the "inexpensive" parts first and then move towards the major parts." I asked him did they inspect the engine and He said "That would be thousands of dollars in repairs but bring the car by and we'll look at it and credit you." This leads me to believe that they knew all along what the issue was but just wanted to make easy money replacing parts that weren't faulty until they eventually got to the problem. The Intake Camshaft Adjuster needed to replaced. Go figure! Also note that the original rotors they replaced were cross drilled. The rotors they installed on my vehicle weren't. When I asked Tony about it He said "It doesn't make a difference because your car isn't a AMG or high performance car." Overall a horrible experience. Update: 12/12/18I received a voicemail from Tony saying he seen the review and wanted to know why I didn't come by to let him look at my vehicle again. I let my mom call him back because at this point I feel taken advantage of and don't trust him. My mom called and he basically said he would have given me a credit if I brought my car back. She questioned him about why they didn't inspect the engine to begin with and He said the same thing he told me before. I don't appreciate wasting my time or money with no positive results. Again, a horrible experience with poor customer service.

I rarely leave negative reviews, but I feel it's important to share my experience so others can make an informed decision.We brought our BMW to Integrity 1st Automotive on Belmont in Lakewood because it was idling rough and showing drivetrain malfunction warnings after rodents had chewed some wiring (which had already been professionally repaired before we arrived).After dropping the car off, communication was very slow and we had to repeatedly call for updates. After a few days, we were told the vehicle needed spark plugs, an ignition coil, and an induction service. When that didn't fix the issue, we were told to replace the turbocharger boost sensor, then the mass air flow sensor. After spending $1,409, the car was still doing the exact same thing as when we brought it in.Near the end of the process, the shop found a hole in the intake tubing and wanted to charge $600 to repair it. While they did agree to split that cost, it added to our frustration because it felt like the real problem was never clearly diagnosed from the start. We were even told to "drive it through the weekend and see if it works," which did not inspire confidence.To make matters worse, when we picked the car up we discovered the windshield washer tubing had been broken off, something that worked fine before the visit. We also noticed new rattling/chattering noises under the hood as if it wasn't properly put back together.At this point, we are left with an unreliable vehicle, a large bill, and the original problem unresolved. This experience did not reflect the level of integrity the company name promises, and we felt frustrated and taken advantage of throughout the process.I hope management will take this feedback seriously and improve diagnostic practices, communication, and quality control so other customers don't have the same experience we did.Lastly, I received an email from the Vice President, Andrew Wheeler, checking in a week after we got our car back.  Basically checking in to see how the car was running.  I wrote him back a lengthy email listing all that had happened and asking for them to make it right.  I never heard back.  I also called and left a message.  Never heard back.  Think twice before taking your car to Integrity 1st in Lakewood.

I truly wanted this to be a positive experience, but unfortunately it was not.I brought my 2017 Volkswagen Tiguan (about 79,000 miles) to Austin's Automotive Specialists in November 2025 for an oil leak. After their inspection, I approved additional repairs and paid $2,006.64, picking up the car on Nov. 17.Within days, the leak was worse. Instead of one leak, I now had two, along with a strong burning-oil smell while driving. I returned on Nov. 21 and was told it was just leftover oil and that everything had been cleaned.That same evening, I discovered the car was leaking from three different places.I brought the car back again on Dec. 2. After no communication all day, I called and was told they found another leaking gasket and wanted an additional $400. I was also told that "oil leaks are tricky on Volkswagens," but a properly diagnosed repair should not turn one leak into multiple new leaks in a matter of days.At this point, I declined further work. I paid over $2,000 and ended up with a car in worse condition than when I brought it in. On 06/06/2019 my family vehicle failed to start after stalling in a parking garage on the UIW campus. The repair technician was contacted over the phone and dispatched to my location. He assessed the vehicle problem and assured me that he would be able to make the necessary repairs on the spot. The fuel pump had gone out and required replacing. After 3 1/2 hours of work in the parking garage, the technician guaranteed the serviceability of the vehicle from his repairs. I payed, in cash, $250 for the job. Upon payment, I departed the parking garage and went home for the evening. After pulling into my driveway, the check engine light came on. I then messaged the technician, "On the way home I got a check engine light and it is not feeling right. I can get the light scanned tomorrow." He responded, "Let me know what the code is for. It is probably the emission code for the gas cap." The car was brought to the local mechanic to get the engine code scanned. The local mechanics informed me that not only was this a "botched fuel pump job" that needed to be redone, but the fuel tank was secured with zip ties. I forwarded the report from the local mechanic to the mobile technician and requested a refund to cover the cost of having the fuel pump job redone by my local mechanic. He replied," that's not how that works. The tank doesn't have to be removed neither does the fuel pump. That's OK if you want to have someone else repair it but I spent a lot of time on that repair especially that tank strap that would not fit back on." I replied, "zip tying the gas tank back on is not acceptable in my book. Look man, your work is guaranteed for 60 days. It was not an easy job but I have to pay to have it redone. I cant have my gas tank wobbling around all over the place. I just want a refund to get it installed correctly." The mobile technician has failed to respond to any forms of contact made by me. The vehicle was repaired by my local technician, properly.

So i pay him the half. 12pm comes the next day and its the same excuse as last night and he has to take a motor. I'm already getting kind of upset at this point being jerked around time after time but i still say ok and give him more time. 2 oclock comes around and now an electrician is at his house putting in an electrical box so he had to wait for that to be done and he would come bring my car to my house his self at 7 because he said to make sure he has a good enough time to make sure everything was done right.  So i say ok again. 7pm and still no word. So i call and I'm pretty upset at this point and say i just want to get my car and pick it up. If he wasn't done yet i was just going to tow it.  This guy has the audacity to say. Possession is 9/10s of the law like he was going to keep my car because he thought i wouldn't pay the rest of what i owed him. We got off the phone so he could get back to work and we were both pretty frustrated. After that i calmed down and just texted that i would give him Another day. He later said on text at 9pm that he would just bring my car in the MORNING the next day. The next day comes and still no car. I call and text about 15 times in the morning with no answer so I'm really concerned at this point. Finally when i get fed up with the games i call the local police who said to call them when I'm 5 min away from his shop and they would send some officers to help me get my property. So i text him for the last time after all the previous texts and tell him if my car is not here at my house by 2pm I'm coming over with the police. As soon as i texted him that he texted back saying that he SLEPT IN and didn't see all my calls and texts till just then and to give him a couple more hours and he would bring it to me. Now, just as a background, I have five children and two jobs and was stressed out because my wife was out of town, so I agreed without thinking too much.He said the MAF sensor was $145.00 and the IAT Sensor was $75.00. In reality, they were both about $50, but I was getting the kids out the door to school so I didn't have time to check it right then. He left, got the sensors, put them in, and said the light was off. He said if it came back on, just let him know and he would take care of it, I went to pay him and he insisted I pay with Zelle instead of through the app he asked for. I paid him and he left, saying that he would be back later that afternoon with a different computer to program the Key fob for the car.I drove the car for 10 minutes and the check engine light came back on, so I tried calling him, it went to voicemail, so I texted him and the messages turned green on my iphone - he blocked me.So, here are the takeaways:-He did not give me the old parts from the car, so I am not even sure if he replaced them-He said lifetime warranty on parts and labor but blocked me the same day as the repair-He said he would return to finish the job, and never did-And, it goes without saying he overcharged for the parts and the labor.
